def: To make little or less in a moral sense; to speak of in adepreciatory or contemptuous way. T. Jefferson.
In France, an official government publication bound in yellowcovers.
Like a lubber; clumsy.A great lubberly boy. Shak.

That process by which a number of isolated facts are broughtunder one conception, or summed up in a general proposition, as whenKepler discovered that the various observed positions of the planetMars were points in an ellipse. "The colligation of facts." Whewell.Colligation is not always induction, but induction is alwayscolligation. J. S. Mill.
An instrument for ascertaining the weight or density of air andgases.

A california woodpecker (Melanerpes formicivorus), noted forits habit of inserting acorns in holes which it drills in trees. Theacorns become infested by insect larvæ, which, when grown, areextracted for food by the bird.
A poem of eight lines. [Obs.] Selden
